intro: I am interested in the remote AWS Data Engineer role with Unisys. My background aligns strongly with AWS-based data engineering, Python, SQL, ETL/ELT pipelines, data quality, Redshift-backed analytics, and production data workflow support.
body: At Citi, I built automated Python and Pandas ETL pipelines that ingested and standardized telemetry from 6,000+ enterprise endpoints. I also designed AWS-oriented data platform patterns using S3 landing zones, Glue-based ETL, and Redshift analytics to support forecasting, reporting, and operational decision-making.

Your role's focus on S3, Glue, Redshift, Lake Formation-style data governance, Lambda/Step Functions patterns, batch and streaming architectures, SQL optimization, data modeling, and documentation maps well to my practical experience. I have built large-scale data workflows with Python, SQL, PySpark, Spark, AWS services, data validation routines, and production troubleshooting discipline.

I would bring a senior delivery mindset to this engagement: reliable ingestion, clean transformations, well-structured data assets, performance-aware storage design, and maintainable documentation for the teams who will operate the platform. I have not worked directly in utility-domain IEC CIM standards, but my experience with regulated banking data, infrastructure telemetry, and enterprise integration gives me a strong foundation for learning that domain quickly.
conclusion: I would welcome the opportunity to support Unisys and its federal client with reliable AWS data engineering delivery.
